title: The Brain That Changes Itself
subtitle: Stories of Personal Triumph from the Frontiers of Brain Science
author: Norman Doidge
themes: The brain is not fixed after childhood — it can change throughout life (neuroplasticity)
Neurons that fire together wire together — and neurons that fire apart wire apart
Brain areas can be remapped — functions can shift to undamaged regions
Mental activity changes brain structure — thinking physically reshapes the brain
Neuroplasticity offers hope for conditions previously considered untreatable: stroke, brain injury, learning disabilities, chronic pain
The discovery of neuroplasticity is one of the most important scientific revolutions of the modern era
excerpt: The idea that the brain is like a machine with specialized parts that cannot change was once the dominant view in neuroscience. But the discovery of neuroplasticity — the brain's ability to change its own structure and function in response to mental experience — has overturned this view. The brain is not a machine. It is a living, growing organ that can rewire itself throughout life.
